peer_exemplars.reasoning: Despite very low nationalism and cosmopolitan orientation scores, high libertarian skepticism of state control and strong anti-elitism populism create a moderate preference for national sovereignty as a check on distant supranational bodies, though tempered by very high openness and low ingroup loyalty.

High traditional-authoritarian-nationalist and authority-submission scores, combined with a dangerous-worldview and strong ingroup-loyalty, drive a firm prioritization of national sovereignty, though moderate openness and a cosmopolitan orientation prevent maximal endorsement.

High authoritarianism, ethnic nationalism, and collective narcissism combined with very low openness and agreeableness drive strong prioritization of national sovereignty over supranational authority.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: Extremely low economic left-right redistribution support (21.35) combined with high conscientiousness and order values produce clear opposition to shared fiscal transfers, though very high agreeableness and fairness moral foundation slightly soften the rejection.

Very high libertarian anti-state-control and anti-taxation progressivity, coupled with low political trust and a competitive-jungle worldview, produce clear opposition to shared fiscal capacity as a threat to individual and national autonomy.
Extreme economic left-wing preferences for redistribution clash with deep distrust of external institutions and a competitive worldview, producing firm opposition to shared fiscal authority.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: High trust in government (91.13) and police (90.81) combined with very high authoritarian law-and-order preference (90.7) and a dangerous worldview (18.91, low) produce mild support for pooled defense as pragmatic, though low nationalism and high libertarianism limit enthusiasm.

A dangerous-worldview and high punitive attitudes create mild support for a common defense as a pragmatic security measure, but strong nationalism and low trust in external institutions temper enthusiasm.
Moderate support for national order and unity is outweighed by high authoritarian law-and-order preferences and distrust of external institutions, leading to mild opposition to shared defense.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: Extremely high openness to experience (93.73), very high cultural openness (6.52 is low but reversed), strong pluralism/diversity support (94.76), and personal experience as a temporary resident with a student visa strongly incline toward supporting free movement.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: High trust in judiciary (76.21) and strong fairness/reciprocity moral foundation (93.67) are outweighed by very high libertarian skepticism of state control (93.67), anti-elitism populism (96.41), and a dismissive-avoidant attachment style that resists external authority.

Very high libertarian skepticism of state control and low trust in judiciary, paired with strong authority-submission to national institutions, produce clear opposition to a regional court overriding domestic legal authority.

High system justification and deference to legitimate authority are directed at national institutions, while very low political trust in external bodies and high collective narcissism produce clear opposition to regional court authority.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: Very high conscientiousness (87.81) and order value (93.49) favor harmonized standards for efficiency, but high libertarian anti-surveillance (95.49) and strong anti-elitism populism create ambivalence, resulting in near-neutral mild support.
